key_themes: Analyst price target upgrades and consensus Buy ratings
Pending Union Pacific-Norfolk Southern merger with regulatory scrutiny
Upcoming Q1 2026 earnings with expected modest EPS and revenue growth
Domestic rail production contracts and union job protection agreements
Operational reliability focus and positive PR initiatives (e.g. Big Boy tour)
summary: Investor sentiment toward UNP is positive ahead of its Q1 2026 earnings on April 23, with consensus estimates calling for $2.85 EPS (up 5.6% YoY) and ~2.2% revenue growth to $6.16B.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/union-pacific-report-q1-earnings-150700306.html)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/union-pacific-unp-projected-to-post-earnings-on-thursday-2026-04-16/) Analysts maintain a Buy consensus with an average 12-month price target near $272 (from current levels around $248), supported by multiple recent upgrades citing strong volumes.[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/UNP/)[[4]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/UNP) The proposed $85B merger with Norfolk Southern (shareholder-approved, pending STB review with expected close in early 2027) is a major long-term theme, promising coast-to-coast efficiency and supply chain benefits while drawing regulatory attention and competitor comments about market control.[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proposed_merger_between_Union_Pacific_and_Norfolk_Southern)[[6]](https://www.up.com/news/service/union-pacific-norfolk-merger-reliability-260302) Recent wins like a long-term domestic steel rail contract, union agreements protecting jobs, and heritage steam locomotive PR are reinforcing stability and domestic focus.[[7]](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/UNP/)[[8]](https://tickernerd.com/stock/unp-forecast/)
sources: 15+ recent news articles and press releases (Union Pacific investor site, Yahoo Finance, Zacks, Business Wire), Multiple analyst notes with recent PT raises (Benchmark to $275, Raymond James to $285, Citigroup to $285), 40+ X posts (primarily earnings calendar mentions, merger discussion, technical analysis, and pre-earnings positioning)
